channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Rapid Safety Upgrades for Air Traffic Control Systems on Horizon
item.description: Elon Musk announces rapid safety upgrades for air traffic control systems with FAA support, citing recent system failure and deadly midair collision near Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Digma Preemptive Observability Analysis engine tackles AI code bugs
item.description: Digma has unveiled Preemptive Observability Analysis, an observability engine designed to reduce bugs introduced by AI code generation and other coding issues. The commercial version, introduced February 5, follows a limited-capability free version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Windows 11’s Start menu search gets new, clearer labels, as Microsoft tries to avoid EU regulation trouble
item.description: Microsoft is updating Windows 11’s Start menu search in the EEA (European Economic Area) to comply with EU rules, separating on-device and Bing web search while adding more user control options.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: US Postal Service resumes accepting parcels from China and Hong Kong hours after saying it wouldn't
item.description: Move followed US introduction of tariffs on Chinese imports and removal of long-standing duty-exemption rule, but is no longer in effect
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Adobe's AI assistant can now decipher contract jargon in your PDFs
item.description: Acrobat AI Assistant can now handle contracts to make sure you don’t miss important details obscured in complex language.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Databricks acquires BladeBridge to aid data warehouse migrations
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has acquired data platform modernization software provider BladeBridge for an undisclosed sum to help its new customers move from rival data warehouses, such as Teradata, Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, and Microsoft SQL Server, to its lakehouse built atop Databricks S
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Smart Traffic Enforcement: Kazakhstan’s Qorgau System in Action
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Energy-saving scam uses Elon Musk’s name – Here’s the truth
item.description: A new scam using Elon Musk's name is making the rounds, promoting "energy-saving" devices and falsely linking the Tesla and SpaceX leader to the products.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple's iOS vulnerability exposes iPhones to stealthy hacker attacks
item.description: Hackers sometimes find ways to exploit iPhones, and Apple recently discovered a vulnerability in iOS that may have been exploited for over a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Another Chinese company flexes AI muscle
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Warehouse robot uses AI to play real-life Tetris to handle more than ever before
item.description: A multipurpose robotic system promises to transform how items are stacked onto pallets, aiming to maximize space utilization and reduce shipping costs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
